name: Sauteed Catfish & Ginger Peach Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1 Can 16 oz sliced drained peaches or 1/2 cup peeled, chopped fresh peaches
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium bowl, combine flour, thyme, salt, and pepper.
2. Rinse the fish and pat dry. Dredge each fillet in the flour mixture; shake off excess and lay the fillets in a single layer on a plate.
3. Heat oil and butter in a 10- to 12-inch frying pan over medium heat. Add pecans and stir until fragrant (1-2 minutes). Transfer nuts to paper towel to drain.
4. Add fish to the pan, without overlapping. Cook in batches if necessary. Cook until crisp and golden on the bottom, about 4 minutes; then turn fillets over and cook until fish is opaque and tender in the center of the thickest area, another 4 minutes.
5. Transfer fish briefly to paper towel to drain. Arrange on plates and spoon ginger peach sauce over each serving. Sprinkle with pecans.
Ginger Peach Sauce:
1. Melt margarine in a small saucepan over medium heat. Add ginger and stir for 1 minute.
2. If using fresh peaches, add them and cook, stirring occasionally, until they are fork tender, about 2 minutes. If using canned peaches, add them with the preserves; stir until the jam melts.
3. Stir in ketchup, white wine vinegar, and hot pepper sauce (Tabasco). Transfer the mixture to a blender or food processor and whirl until the peaches are coarsely chopped. The sauce can be cooled and refrigerated up to 2 weeks.
4. To serve, warm the sauce in a saucepan over medium heat, stirring occasionally.
